name: Park Avenue Rochester Hotel
detail: Pros: Modern, clean, king bed, soft sheet, bathtub, walking distance to Star Vista(lots of food choices), roomview of greenery surroundings Cons: Staff can be better trained. Had a very bad experience with a lady counter staff when my room card was malfunction and could not activate the lift to go up to my room floor. When I went back to counter for the second time to ask why the card was still not working, she decided to walk me to the lift to make sure card is working. Instead the card was still not working and she had to activate the lift with her staff pass. It then worked but before I can thank her she walk away, seeming to show me her displeasure for causing inconvenience to her that she has to walk to the lift with me. This is the first time I experience such a bad service in a Singapore hotel for my staycation. I hope the hotel can consider re-training their staff to make sire this does not happen to other guests again.

name: YOTEL Singapore Orchard Road
detail: We are so happy that we chose to spend 5 night a brand new Yotel. *The staff- Very polite caring and helpful. (the best staff we ever had in a hotel) They helped us with planning, adjusted for us the menu to our vegan twists, even cheak and printed for us information about an event in the city! *The location - Perfect location very close to ION mall and MRT station. we mostly used buses. it is nicer to see the city this way (bus stop is even closer and located in front of Thailand embassy) we also liked the hotel located on the beginning of Orchard street. it is very easy to take a bus to see all the street. Since it's a one way street you can get out each time in the stop you like. * The room - small as most rooms in Singapore hotels but designed very smart and functional! we felt like in a space-ship with cool view on the city skyline. * facilities - 10th floor is Amazing! the gym is very good and new! The restaurant is amazing with very good prices. We got a 50% discount for breakfast for choosing to eat only fruits and veges in the morning :) kitchen is open till 22:30 and we used to get nice evening vegan fruit and veges salad that was adjusted for us! Thank you so much for the warm hospitality. we relay appreciate every one of you! You are areal Pros! Best Regards, Daniel & Sarit

name: Copthorne King's Hotel Singapore
detail: My fiance and I stayed at this hotel, and really loved our experience. The hotel staff were very friendly, and helpful. The rooms were clean, modern, and the beds were extremely comfortable. The location was great, and we were able to walk to many places. They also provide a free shuttle to the main areas in Singapore, which worked out great for us. For our few days at this hotel, we felt it really became our home. We generally don't use the fitness facilities at hotels, but felt like they really hit the mark with theirs, so took advantage of the pool/sauna/steam room. The pool and hot tub were also great, and most of the time we were able to enjoy them to ourselves. Any other guests we would run into were very friendly. We would definitely stay here again, especially since we found it so affordable compared to many other hotels in Singapore. Overall, great experience!

name: Robertson Quay Hotel
detail: If you are visiting Singapore & you are looking a cheap hotel that is nice & clean. I would really recommend Robertson Quay hotel to anybody who is going to visit Singapore. Although the room is a little bit smaller but cleanliness is 'five starred' & the location is pretty strategic. Behind this hotel you can see a river and there's this pedestrian walkway which leads to Clarke Quay, The famous spot for party goers (5-10 mins walk) & from Clarke Quay you can easily get an access to the public transportation like Buses & MRT.

name: BEAT. 1932 Hostel @ Chinatown
detail: I will definitely stay here again when I come to Singapore.

name: Dorsett Changi City Singapore
detail: Love the kitchenette, perfect for staycation visits with your loved ones. The room was large (compared to majority of hotels in Singapore), clean and very comfortable. Room also features a full-sized window view of the Singapore Expo area. Location of hotel is just a 5 minutes walk away from the Expo MRT station and no lack of amenities as Changi City Point is right beside it. Amazing value for money with the last minute promotion deal.

name: Carlton City Hotel Singapore
detail: We've stayed in around a dozen 5 star business hotels in Singapore and this is our favourite. The hotel is modern and chic and everything is perfectly clean. The location is brilliant, 100m from a station, 50m from a supermarket (with a 7 day pharmacy inside) and 100m from a hawkers centre. The staff are efficient, warm and professional.

name: M Social Hotel Singapore
detail: Loved the design of this hotel, feels luxurious for its price and has little technological touches that make everything easier and get you into the Singapore vibe. The rooms are tiny but that’s very common in Singapore (as in many major cities around the world). Everything is very clean and designed to make the best out of the small space. The staff were very nice (and the robot who acts as room service was cool as well). I didn’t love the breakfast, not many options for plant-based eaters, but if you’re used to having eggs, meat and dairy in the morning you should be fine. The location is great - by a nice section of the river with many cafes, bars and restaurants. It is about 15 min walking distance from the nearest MRT station but the hotel also offers a free shuttle service to select locations in the city which makes it very easy to get around. All in all the hotel was great and we will definitely return to it next time we’re in Singapore.

name: Siloso Beach Resort Sentosa
detail: We had prior to our arrival read the comments of this hotel and found them not very positive overall. Our experience upon arrival was something completely different. Staff was very friendly, we were in awe after seeing the pool area and completely fell in love with this hotel. The view from our room overlooked the sea through a window the size of a wall. The breakfast buffet was delicious. The hotel is located right by the beach and close to tram and train stops.Sure the hotel room itself, mainly the bathroom might need a bit of an update, but it was clean and housekeeping kept it that way everyday. However, the pool, view, breakfast and all other positive aspects made up for the slight outdated look. A young, active couple as ourselves do not spend much time in the room anyway. We have stayed in other hotels in Singapore city before, which are of a higher standard, but still prefer Siloso Beach Resort to any of those. We will absolutely stay here again next time we visit Singapore.

name: Hotel 1900 @ Chinatown
detail: Overall, it was a fantastic stay and we highly recommend it.
